summary: Linked Crazy Hand by splitting the previous monolithic ftCh_Init translation unit into many per-action ftCrazyHand source/header files, marking those objects Matching in configure.py, and adding precise GALE01 split ranges for each new unit. The PR preserved the same address span from 0x80155E18 through 0x8015BD20 while redistributing .text/.data/.sdata2 across files such as ftCh_Wait1_0, ftCh_FingerBeam, ftCh_BackAirplane*, ftCh_TagGrab, ftCh_GrabUnk1_B174, and ftCh_TagCancel. Bot metrics reported +24,328 linked code bytes, +2,432 linked data bytes, and 293 new matches, with apparent broken matches mostly because functions moved out of ftCh_Init into their own matching units.
what_changed: Changed Crazy Hand in configure.py from one NonMatching object, melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c, to a sequence of Matching objects covering ftCh_Init plus 35 newly split ftCrazyHand action files.

Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so the former ftCh_Init.c span from .text 0x80155E18-0x8015BD20 is divided into per-file ranges for Wait1_0, Entry, Damage_0, Sweep, SweepWait, Walk, Drill, RockCrush, Poke, FingerBeam, BackAirplane, BackCrush, BackDisappear, Wait1_1, Grab, Cancel, Squeeze/Squeezing, Throw, Slam, TagRockPaper, FingerGun2, TagGrab, GrabUnk1_B174, capture states, thrown state, and TagCancel.
Moved most code out of ftCh_Init.c: the file shows 2767 deleted lines and only 30 added lines, while many new action-specific .c and .h files were added under src/melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand.
Added headers for each split action file, generally using address-based include guards such as GALE01_1592D4 and prototypes annotated with address comments like /* 1592D4 */.

Introduced/used action-state entry, Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll functions in the split files; examples in the excerpt include ftCh_Init_801592D4, ftCh_BackAirplane1_Anim, ftCh_BackAirplane1_IASA, ftCh_BackAirplane1_Phys, and ftCh_BackAirplane1_Coll.
Added ftCrazyHand/ftCh_TagCancel.h include to ftCommon/ftCo_Fall.c, implying cross-file dependencies needed explicit headers after the split.
Touched ftmotionstates.c and several existing fighter/Master Hand files with small include or reference updates so the new Crazy Hand symbols resolve after linking.
smart_moves: Kept the original function order and address boundaries visible in splits.txt while carving one large object into many smaller matching objects, making the link step auditable by address.
Used one action/motion-oriented file per Crazy Hand behavior, mirroring the fighter action-state organization already common in the tree and making future naming/refinement work more local.
Converted the large ftCh_Init.c header burden into per-file headers with prototypes, reducing reliance on a catch-all init header.

The bot report's new and broken match lists show the move was a relinking/repartitioning operation: functions became 100% in their new units while disappearing from the old ftCh_Init unit, which is expected for a split.
Maintained data-section ranges as part of the split, including .data for ftCh_Wait1_0 and ftCh_GrabUnk1_B174 and continuous .sdata2 coverage through ftCh_TagCancel, avoiding a code-only split.
decomp_lessons: For large boss/fighter files, split by contiguous address ranges and immediately update both configure.py and config/GALE01/splits.txt; otherwise symbols may match locally but fail to link in the project layout.

When functions move out of a monolithic file, expect match-report 'broken matches' in the old unit paired with 'new matches' in the new units. Treat this as relocation evidence, not necessarily a regression.
Cross-action helper dependencies become real header dependencies after splitting; add the specific ftCh_* headers at call sites instead of relying on the old aggregate header.
Headers in this PR preserve address comments and address-derived include guards, which are useful while many symbols still have provisional names like fn_801588B8 or ftCh_Init_80156AD8.
Small padding or stack-shape artifacts may remain in matched functions; the excerpt shows a local dummy array, u8 _[8], in ftCh_BackAirplane3_Anim, a common matching tactic when evidence supports it.
naming_conventions: Crazy Hand files use the ftCh_ prefix and action names in PascalCase-ish filenames, for example ftCh_BackAirplane1.c, ftCh_FingerBeam.c, ftCh_TagGrab.c, and ftCh_CaptureDamageCrazyHand.c.
Action callbacks follow the fighter convention of Action_Anim, Action_IASA, Action_Phys, and Action_Coll, such as ftCh_BackAirplane2_Anim and ftCh_BackAirplane2_Coll.
Initializer/state-change functions are still largely address-suffixed, for example ftCh_Init_801592D4, ftCh_Init_801594D4, and ftCh_Init_80159670.
Some helper names remain provisional or address-based, including ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B850 and fn_801588B8 as shown by the bot report; no evidence in the PR text explains final intended names.
Header guards use GALE01 address tags such as GALE01_1592D4, matching the first function/address in the header.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marked every new ftCrazyHand split object as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py rather than leaving the original ftCh_Init.c as NonMatching.
Used splits.txt to assign exact .text start/end ranges for every split unit from 0x80155E18 to 0x8015BD20, preserving link order.
Tracked .sdata2 boundaries per new file; the old ftCh_Init .sdata2 range 0x804DA050-0x804DA1F8 became many smaller ranges ending with ftCh_TagCancel at 0x804DA1F0-0x804DA1F8.

Kept action-state boilerplate explicit in C: Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls with numeric motion IDs, ftAnim_8006EBA4, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, cmd_vars checks, and BossLib/common fighter helper calls.
Included common matching support headers such as placeholder.h, platform.h, common_structs.h, dolphin/mtx.h, and MetroTRK/intrinsics.h in the new source files where needed.
review_feedback: No substantive human review comments were present in the slice.
One collaborator review approved the PR with an empty body.

The decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 67.77% (+0.03%, +1172 bytes), linked code 39.86% (+0.63%, +24328 bytes), matched data 39.65% (+0.20%, +2416 bytes), and linked data 33.46% (+0.20%, +2432 bytes).
Bot report listed 293 new matches and 254 broken matches; the broken matches were concentrated in the old ftCh_Init unit after functions moved into new units.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename provisional Crazy Hand helpers like fn_801588B8 or ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B174.
Inspect PRs around ftCrazyHand/types.h or ftMasterHand/types.h for struct-field naming of ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs and mv.ch fields used here.
Search for later cleanup of placeholder.h/platform.h includes in ftCrazyHand split files.
Check whether later work replaces numeric motion state IDs such as 0x16F, 0x170, and 0x171 with enum names.
